answer:
Well, to be honest, I prefer doing homework alone over using online help. I find that when I'm working on my own, I can really dive deep into the material and focus without any distractions. You know, it's like getting into a flow state where everything just clicks. For example, I remember last semester when I was tackling a challenging math assignment. Instead of searching for answers online, I dedicated a few quiet evenings solely to practicing those problems. 

I think one reason I lean towards solo study is that it allows me to develop my problem-solving skills more effectively. When I encounter a tough question, I have to figure it out by myself, which enhances my critical thinking. Actually, I also feel a sense of accomplishment after figuring things out independently—it boosts my confidence. 

That said, I do see the value in online resources. They're great for quick clarifications or when I'm stuck, but I try to minimize reliance on them. Sometimes, it's tempting to just look up the answer, but I remind myself of the long-term benefits of really engaging with the material. 

So, in conclusion, while online help can be useful in certain situations, I believe that studying alone helps me learn more comprehensively and become more self-sufficient. It's about finding the right balance, but for now, I enjoy the quiet moments when I can really focus on my homework without any external influences.
title: Do you like doing homework alone or using online help? Why choose this way?
